slides.h1:
Robots on the line
from motion to outcome.
slides.lead: Demonstration of the template. Every line is sample (dummy) text and exists only to show the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This holds the event name, a one-line subtitle and the meta row at the bottom. Wrap one word in em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared goal on the floor.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses colored initials instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Cell status, cycle data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Arms, AGVs and vision cells run every shift. The value shows up only once they share one signal.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, strong illustration, short caption. Good for a punchline or a chapter.

items: fewer unplanned cell stops after predictive tuning
saved daily on manual robot teach and rework
higher throughput per cell from path optimization
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: the effect on the line is what counts.
Start where you already have signal and feel the pain most.
A small pilot with one metric beats a grand plan with none.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a caption, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The cell does not wait for a jam, it predicts it.
bullets: Force, position and vision from each robot.
What preceded past faults and misfeeds.
Service into the window that costs least.
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same robot cell, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: We fix the cell only once it stops. Costly and unpredictable.
Service by the calendar. Better, but often too early.
Service by real condition. Lowest total cost.
Critical cells predictive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a smart cell.
cards: Robot data in real time, close to the source.
Live view of cell state and deviations.
Flags odd motion before a person would.
Raw data into a daily brief for leadership.
Signals stay inside your network, under control.
A team that understands and trusts the cells.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for a tool or capability overview.

items: Gains slow down, it pays to finish the basics first.
Tools improve fast and reshape settled routines.
Automation takes on layout design, not just motion.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: The winner is not the cell with the most sensors, but the team that turns signal into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to demonstrate the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every cell has the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how success is judged.
On critical steps a person always decides.
slides.notes: Plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.h1: A report from cell data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build the daily brief live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
